evals.name: google-meet-controller
evals.prompt: Build a Stream Deck Plus tool that controls Google Meet. I want three buttons: one to toggle my mic mute (shows 🎤 when live, 🔇 when muted), one to toggle my camera (shows 📷 when on, 🚫 when off), and one to hang up. It should detect whether Chrome is focused on a Meet call using osascript and update the button states. When I'm not in a call, the buttons should look dimmed/grey.
evals.expected_output: Working index.js + package.json. Uses osascript to check Meet state in Chrome. Three buttons with correct icons and colors. Polls every 2 seconds. Uses Block Artifactory registry.
assertions: package.json includes @elgato-stream-deck/node as a dependency
package.json includes sharp as a dependency
osascript is used to detect Chrome/Meet state
Three separate button indices are used for mic, camera, and hang up
Button rendering uses sharp to produce raw RGB buffers
setInterval polling is present to continuously update button state
deck.on('down') handler is present for button presses
Different colors/icons used for active vs inactive (in-call vs not-in-call) states

evals.name: pomodoro-timer
evals.prompt: Make a Stream Deck button that's a Pomodoro timer. One button on my Stream Deck Plus shows the countdown (25:00, 24:59, etc). Click it to start or pause. When time runs out the button turns red and shows DONE. Click again to reset. No external APIs needed, just Node.js timers.
evals.expected_output: Working index.js + package.json. Button updates every second showing MM:SS countdown. Green when running, yellow when paused, red when done. Click toggles start/pause, double-click or long-press resets. Uses Block Artifactory registry.
assertions: package.json includes @elgato-stream-deck/node as a dependency
package.json includes sharp as a dependency
setInterval or setTimeout used for per-second countdown
MM:SS format countdown is rendered onto the button
Button color changes based on timer state (at least two distinct colors for running/done)
deck.on('down') handler toggles start/pause
Reset logic is present (either on DONE click or separate mechanism)
No external fetch/HTTP calls — pure local logic

evals.name: system-volume-encoder
evals.prompt: I want to use the dial/encoder on my Stream Deck Plus to control my Mac's system volume. Rotating clockwise increases volume, counter-clockwise decreases it. Pressing the encoder toggles mute. The LCD strip should show the current volume percentage. Use osascript to get and set system volume.
evals.expected_output: Working index.js + package.json. Encoder rotate event changes volume via osascript. Encoder press toggles mute. LCD strip shows current volume. Debounced so fast spinning doesn't flood osascript calls. Uses Block Artifactory registry.
assertions: package.json includes @elgato-stream-deck/node as a dependency
package.json includes sharp as a dependency
deck.on('rotate') handler is present for encoder rotation
osascript is used to get or set system volume
Rotation is debounced or throttled to avoid flooding osascript calls
Encoder press (deck.on 'down' with encoder type) toggles mute
fillLcd or equivalent is called to update the LCD strip
Volume percentage is shown on the LCD display

evals.name: window-layout-launcher
evals.prompt: Build a Stream Deck Plus tool with buttons for different window layouts I use throughout my day. Button 1 is 'Focus mode' — closes Slack, moves Chrome to fill the left half of my screen, moves iTerm to the right half. Button 2 is 'Meeting mode' — opens Zoom, moves it to center screen at 1200x800, moves my notes app (Obsidian) to the left third. Button 3 is 'Communication' — opens Slack, opens Chrome to slack.com, tiles them side by side. Each button should show the layout name and a relevant icon. Use AppleScript via osascript to move and resize windows.
evals.expected_output: Working index.js + package.json. Three buttons each triggering a different window arrangement via osascript/AppleScript. AppleScript moves/resizes named application windows using 'set bounds'. Apps are opened if not running. Uses Block Artifactory registry.
assertions: package.json includes @elgato-stream-deck/node as a dependency
package.json includes sharp as a dependency
Three distinct buttons are defined for Focus, Meeting, and Communication layouts
osascript or execSync is used to run AppleScript
AppleScript 'set bounds' or equivalent is used to position/resize windows
Apps are activated or opened if not already running
Each button has a distinct label and icon identifying the layout
No external npm packages beyond @elgato-stream-deck/node and sharp are required
